extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb sprinted from Bennett's slipstream on uphill finish with 1.4km crash splitting field, taking stage and race lead on bonus seconds.
tactical_patterns: EWAN positioned in BENNETT's slipstream then emerged late rather than leading out, exploiting uphill drag finish dynamics.
MITCHELTON-SCOTT burned HAMILTON and MEYER for IMPEY but left him exposed right-side too early on gradient.
DECEUNINCK controlled peloton for BENNETT but 1.4km crash nullified their positioning work with three-kilometer rule protection.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: used Bennett as final leadout on uphill finish rather than positioning independently, shows tactical sophistication
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: shook head and abandoned sprint despite having Ewan's wheel, suggests gradient exceeded his capability
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: negative: went long from right boards with full leadout train but faded badly, poor gradient selection
rider_signals.ROHAN Dennis: neutral: punctured but got back via GEOGHEGAN LOPEZ wheel, mechanical luck issue only
